combination.context_description: Selected visual world: Brutalismo | Camera slot: 1NV - Vertical_Nadir | Use only the selected world mother reference image as the environment source. | Inherit atmosphere from the selected world mother image. | Inherit materials from the selected world mother image. | Inherit lighting from the selected world mother image.
combination.compatibility_reason: User-selected scene mother category from the ranked list. Camera slots reconstruct this visual world from their own viewpoint.
combination.camera_slot_description:
Use a low/nadir-inspired camera looking upward from below the artwork centerline.
Use a wide architectural lens that can read vertical height and room volume without circular fisheye.
Use a strong upward tilt, but keep room planes coherent with the supplied environment mother.
Use the oblique rotation implied by the green perspective plate while preserving the environment mother family.
Use the supplied green silhouette perspective plate as the exact visual contract for the artwork projection, while rebuilding a room that remains recognizably derived from the environment mother.
Keep the artwork and immediate wall contact sharp; allow only natural photographic softness in distant room planes.
Camera object integrity: The artwork must remain the supplied artwork identity and must be placed into the exact perspective shape supplied by the plate. The surrounding room must keep the environment mother room type, material family, spatial scale, light temperature, and furniture density.
Camera negatives: no regularized perspective, no straightened artwork, no artwork substitution.
combination.final_prompt_preview:
Use the three reference images.

Image 1 is a perspective mask only.
The green area is the exact projected plane for the artwork.
The black area is not part of the final image.
The final artwork must occupy the same position, size, tilt, and four-corner silhouette as the green area.

Image 2 is the artwork.
Place Image 2 inside the green plane from Image 1, matching its perspective exactly.

Image 3 is the environment mother.
Preserve its architectural family, room type, wall and floor material family, light temperature, spatial scale, furniture density, openings, and atmosphere.
Do not copy its exact camera angle, crop, or object placement, but the final room must be recognizably derived from Image 3.

Create a realistic interior mockup viewed from below looking upward.
If camera drama conflicts with the green mask, obey the green mask.
If the room reference conflicts with the green mask, adapt only the camera geometry and artwork placement; do not replace the environment mother with a generic invented room.
Do not show the green mask or black background.
Do not turn the artwork into a sculpture.
Do not place the artwork on another wall or in another perspective.
Do not create an unrelated arched room, empty showroom, generic gallery, or alternate architecture unless Image 3 itself supports it.
